name: Adept AI
tagline: AI agents that automate software tasks on your behalf
description: Adept AI raised $415M to build AI agents for software automation before Amazon hired the CEO, co-founders, and 80% of the technical team in a $430M deal.
story: Founded in 2022, Adept AI raised $415 million to develop AI agents capable of taking actions in software — clicking buttons, filling forms, and navigating applications on a user's behalf. The company attracted top AI talent and significant investor interest. But in June 2024, Amazon executed a devastating acqui-hire, offering CEO David Luan and the co-founders positions at Amazon along with a $330 million licensing deal and $100 million in retention bonuses. Approximately 80% of Adept's technical staff followed. The deal was structured to avoid regulatory scrutiny — Amazon technically licensed Adept's technology rather than acquiring the company. What remained was a shell. Notably, four of the five co-founders who joined Amazon had departed within a year.

sources.title: Amazon hires founders from AI startup Adept
Adept investors to be paid back after Amazon hire

review_flags: Deal breakdown of '$330M licensing + $100M retention bonuses' ($430M total) in description/story could not be verified from listed sources. Semafor article says investors will 'roughly recoup their investment' from Amazon-paid funds but does not state the $330M/$100M split. The $430M figure may come from other reporting not cited here.
